turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've just been feeling really miserable for two weeks now - my cough is constant and it's started producing this yellowish-green stuff, and I've had trouble breathing too. It's also made me feel exhausted and feverish, and I've even missed some work because of it.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's been awful - I just feel like I'm constantly struggling to catch my breath and the cough is so annoying. And the fatigue is really getting to me, I feel exhausted all the time.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I've also been feeling unwell at night, and I'm worried about how long this will last.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: Yes, I have been having night sweats, actually. It's like my body is just overheating and I wake up drenched in sweat every night.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: It's been pretty uncomfortable, but I'm not sure what's causing it.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: I've also had some sharp pain on my right side when I breathe deeply or cough, and it's been bothering me a lot.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling, but I have had some difficulty breathing that feels like wheezing, especially after exertion or when I get excited.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: I haven't experienced any nausea or stomach issues, thankfully.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy has been really low, to be honest. I've had trouble getting out of bed some days and even when I do get going, I feel exhausted soon after. As for my appetite, it's actually decreased a bit over the past week or so - I just haven't felt like eating as much as I normally would.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've actually had a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's been intermittent and worse in the evenings. And I've also been having some issues at night besides my cough.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I'm not sure how much I should share about my mom's asthma history or my grandmother's lung cancer diagnosis - it's just a bit sensitive for me.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've been taking cetirizine as needed for my seasonal allergies, and also some over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the cough, but it hasn't really helped much. I have a prescription that I take regularly, but that's about it.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I've been eating pretty normally, but like I said, my appetite has decreased lately so I haven't been feeling as hungry as usual.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: I'm an elementary school teacher, so I'm on my feet all day and try to take breaks when I can. To be honest, lately I've been feeling really tired, even just getting through a workday has been exhausting me.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mom actually has asthma.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: I do, yeah... ever since I've been worried about my own health, I've had a bit of anxiety about my lungs and respiratory stuff.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I drink socially, just one or two glasses of wine on weekends. As for coping with stress, it's been really tough lately - teaching is a high-energy job and dealing with this illness has added to my anxiety.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke and I've never used recreational drugs.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: My mom's asthma history has made me more aware of respiratory issues and it's hard not to worry when something like this happens to me.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient:
I'm so glad I can finally talk about everything. To be honest, it's been really tough for me to keep track of all my symptoms and worries. I've just felt like I'm getting worse instead of better, and it's hard not to think about the worst-case scenarios.

Also, I want to mention that several students in my class have had upper respiratory infections lately, so I'm worried that maybe I caught something from them? And my partner has been feeling a bit under the weather too.
